_comment: Role-based unit stats for the Rommel's Arrival OOB, derived from the CNA Unit Characteristics Charts (German [4.46c], Commonwealth [4.46a]) in the VASSAL mod -- see memory cna-unit-stats-source. Each role notes the chart type code it is taken from. IMPORTANT: this is a CLOSE-ASSAULT-ONLY approximation for the current engine. CPA and Close Assault Off/Def come straight from the charts for infantry/recon; but barrage (artillery), anti-armor (AT), the tank-model close-assault system (rule 15.4), morale, and reinforcement arrival are DEFERRED engine mechanics -- so artillery/AT are given oca 0 (no close-assault punch, they defend only) and tanks a representative close-assault value from their historical models. steps = starting TOE strength (charted Maximum TOE, i.e. full strength).
_aa_air_comment: Two roles added in Phase 3.1 so the Order of Battle stops losing whole categories. 'aa' is the Anti-Aircraft-Type combat unit (rule 3.23); it is a Pure Flak unit (rule 46.17: only AA points -> is_pure_aa, free stacking in Major Cities per 9.16b), and its ground stats here are a FLAGGED PROXY (charted CPA 15, motorized, weak (1)/(1) close assault proxied oca 0/dca 1) pending the per-model Light/Heavy AA rows of [4.47]/[4.48]/[4.49] in Phase 3.2, which override anti_armor for the heavy flak (the 88 / 90-53). 'sgsu' is the Squadron Ground Support Unit (rules 3.21 / 35.0), and in Phase 5.1 it REPLACED the inert 'air' carrier role: the Air Landing Strips and flying-boat Alighting areas that shared that role are no longer units at all but game.state.AirFacility installations (rule 36), so only the SGSU counters are still units. Its stats are the rulebook's where the rulebook states them -- 35.12 'they are vehicles', 'they have no Stacking Point Value' (sp 0), 'no combat strength of any type' (oca/dca 0, is_combat false) -- with ONE FLAGGED PROXY, AND SAY EXACTLY WHICH CELL IT IS: 35.12 says the Capability Point Allowance is 'printed on the counter', we have no scan of an SGSU counter, so cpa 30 is borrowed from the [54.2] Truck Characteristics Chart's MEDIUM TRUCK row -- which prints THREE Capability Point Allowances for a Medium Truck Point, not one (motorizing infantry 20, motorizing guns 15, moving SUPPLIES 30). 30 is the supplies column, the largest of the three, chosen because an SGSU hauls its squadron's stores and equipment rather than troops or guns (35.0: 'SGSU's are the mechanics, trucks, equipment, etc.'). Rule 35.0 licenses the row ('the SGSU counters are considered vehicles (MEDIUM TRUCKS) and have their own Capability Point Allowance printed on the counter'); it does not license the column, which is ours. Nothing moves an SGSU yet, so the proxy is inert -- it goes live the moment 35.13/35.25 arrival-and-transfer orders land, and the counter scan settles it.

CW._engineer_comment: [23.0]/[24.61]/[23.13] The Commonwealth's construction units, and the two rows the OOB was missing entirely. The Construction Chart's footnote f names them: 'the Commonwealth Player's one South African Road Construction Battalion and two New Zealand Railroad Construction Battalions'; 24.61 names the NZRRC pair by number ('the 10th and the 13th') and makes them THE ONLY UNITS IN THE GAME THAT MAY BUILD RAILROAD. is_combat false, oca/dca 0 and sp 0 are the RULE (23.11: engineers 'have no real combat value, nor do they exert Zones of Control... not combat units in any way, shape, or form'). PROXY, FLAGGED: their row of the [4.46a] Commonwealth Unit Characteristics Chart is untranscribed, so CPA 30 / 1 step / MOTORIZED mirrors the CW hq row. The `engineer` field is what they may be USED for -- 23.13: the NZRRC are 'used solely for the construction and repair of Railroads', the 1 S.A. Road Construction unit 'solely for Road work'. The road battalion is SEEDED BUT IDLE: rule 24.5 road construction is not implemented (the map's unfinished-road hexes are untranscribed), so it exists as the rulebook's counter and builds nothing yet.

IT._comment: Italian role stats -- representative (weaker CA, older kit than German); tank/gun MODELS (M13/M14 etc.) come from the [4.48] chart via each unit's model. Flagged for [4.46] Italian Unit Characteristics Chart verification.

_models_comment: Per-model combat ratings from the Tank & Gun Characteristics Charts (4.47/4.48/4.49), extracted this session. A unit may name a 'model' to override its role's combat ratings (only the fields present are overridden; steps/cpa/mobility stay from the role). Tank models set oca/dca/anti_armor/armor_protection/is_tank; gun models set anti_armor/vulnerability; artillery set barrage/vulnerability (25pdr also anti_armor).
_bar_comment: 'bar' = Breakdown Adjustment Rating (rule 21.3), the per-model column shift applied on the [21.38] Breakdown Table BEFORE rolling. Signed integer convention: +N = N columns to the RIGHT (more breakdown, less reliable), -N = N LEFT, 0 = none. Sources: German [4.49], Italian [4.48], and Commonwealth [4.47] Tank & Gun Characteristics Charts (see data/breakdown_rates.json 'bar_by_model' for the full recovered chart, incl. the CW column that had to be read off the PDF scan and the trucks' 54.2 -2). German tanks: all 0 except the early Pz III E (BAR 3, Eve-confirmed on scan p.140) (chart note *: temporarily +1 / 1R until Game-Turn 1/31 -- a turn-gate to honor in the reader, not a flat value). Gun/AT/artillery models carry NO 'bar': towed guns with inherent transport never break down (21.11), a '-' on the chart. DATA ONLY -- no engine code reads 'bar' yet.
_phase3_2_comment: Phase 3.2 (2026-07-17): the 56 weapon-system models that were MISSING from the Tank & Gun charts, transcribed from [4.47] Commonwealth (scan PDF p.138), [4.48] Italian (p.139) and [4.49] German (p.140) via scratchpad/port/phase3-oob-gap.json, each cell cross-checked against its verbatim _chart row there. Engine-read fields only (oca/dca/barrage/anti_armor/armor_protection/vulnerability/is_tank/bar); the charts' AA anti-air rating, CPA and Fuel Rate are not per-model engine inputs (CPA comes from the role, fuel from logistics_rates.json) and stay in the gap doc. Italian and German guns are it_/de_-prefixed so the two nations' identically-named captured pieces (149mm Vichy French) do not collide. These models are inert until an OOB record names one via 'model' (none does yet), so adding them changes no built state. Pz III E is NOW SEEDED (2026-07-18): its [4.49] row was misprinted in the 1979 book and the CNAv2.1.0 VASSAL remaster (images/German Tank and Gun Characteristics Chart.png) read it Armor Prtctn 3 / BAR 0, but Eve visually confirmed Armor-Protection 4 / BAR 3 against scan p.140 -- the prep best-inference reading -- resolving the VASSAL-remaster discrepancy. Like the other Phase-3.2 models it is inert (no OOB record names it yet, so no built state changes) and awaits an OOB reference (likely 5 Le Div ~GT21-26).
